LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF/CROSS DEFENDANT PACIFIC CAPITAL BANK N.A., AMENDED DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF. THE ISSUES OF THE PARTIES' LIABILITIES UNDER THE CONTROL AGREEMENT ARE DIRECTLY ADDRESSED IN THE COMPLAINT, THUS THE DECLARATORY RELIEF ACTION IS DUPLICATIVE. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE. CROSS-COMPLAINANT TO PLEAD THE RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN THE PARTIES THAT GIVES RISE TO A DUTY FROM CROSS-DEFENDANT TO CROSS-COMPLAINANT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MERRIMAN CURHAN FORD & CO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF PRESENTS TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO: THE KNOWLEDGE AND CONDUCT OF CACCHIONE AS TO THE CONTROL AGREEMENT AND TRANSFER OF ACCOUNTS TO M.C.F.; WHETHER SUCH KNOWLEDGE AND CONDUCT CAN BE IMPUTED TO M.C.F.; WHETHER THE ACCOUNTS WERE TRANSFERRED IN THE ORDINARY COURSE OF BUSINESS OR THROUGH COLLUSION BETWEEN CACCHIONE AND DEBRAGGIO. WHILE THERE MAY BE SOME MERIT TO THE CLAIM THAT THE TRANSFER WAS RATIFIED, THIS ALSO PRESENTS TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT REGARDING PLAINTIFF'S KNOWLEDGE OF THE CONVERSION AND EXPRESSED CONSENT TO IT. DEFENDANTS OBJECTIONS 1, 2, 3 ARE OVERRULED. OBJECTION 4 IS SUSTAINED. PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998
DISMISSAL WITH PREJUDICE OF 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT AS TO DEFENDANT MERRIMAN CURHAN FORD & CO.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF/CROSS DEFENDANT PACIFIC CAPITAL BANK N.A., DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. CROSS-COMPLAINANT FAILS TO PLEAD FACTS SUFFICIENT TO ESTABLISH THAT THE RELATIONSHIPS BETWEEN THE PARTIES GIVES RAISE TO A DUTY FROM CROSS-DEFENDANT TO CROSS-COMPLAINANT. NO AUTHORITY SUPPORTS PLAINTIFF'S CONTENTION THAT THE BANK OWED CROSS-COMPLAINANT, WHO WAS NOT A RECIPIENT OF ANY LOAN PROCEEDS, A DUTY TO EXERCISE REASONABLE CARE IN THE UNDERWRITING OF THE LOAN TO DEL BIAGGIO. NO FACTS ARE PLEAD TO SUGGEST IT WOULD HAVE BEEN FORESEEABLE TO THE BANK THAT CROSS-COMPLAINANT WOULD BE DEFRAUDED BY ITS OWN EMPLOYEE (CACCHIONE) THEREBY EXPOSING CROSS-COMPLAINANT TO LIABILITY TO THIRD PARTIES. NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT DEFECT CAN BE CURED BY AMENDMENT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-12-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT MERRIMAN CURHAN FORD & CO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: Michael J. Quinn and Ramiz I Rafeedie of K&L Gates (310.552.5000/415.249.1075) for defendant and moving party; Thomas Robins of Frandzel Robins Bloom & Csato (323.852.1000) for plaintiff and responding party. Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied. Plaintiff presents triable issues of fact as to: the knowledge and conduct of Cacchione as to the control agreement and transfer of accounts to M.C.F.; whether such knowledge and conduct can be imputed to M.C.F.; whether the accounts were transferred in the ordinary course of business or through collusion between Cacchione and DeBraggio. While there may be some merit to the claim that the transfer was ratified, this also presents triable issues of fact regarding plaintiff's knowledge of the conversion and expressed consent to it. Defendants objections 1, 2, 3 are overruled. Objection 4 is sustained. Plaintiff to submit a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

Notice Of Continuance Of Hearing Of Demurrers To Third Amended Cross Complaint Of The Shemano Group
Off Calendar Mar-09-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF PACIFIC CAPITAL BANK N.A., DBA FIRST NATIONAL Notice Of Continuance Of Hearing Of Demurrers To Third Amended Cross Complaint Of The Shemano Group. (Duplicate entry.)(302/CWW)

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-09-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF/CROSS DEFENDANT PACIFIC CAPITAL BANK N.A., DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Appearances: Shari D. Marks, Esq. of Frandzel Robins Bloom & Csato L.C. (415) 788-7400 appearing for Plaintiff/Cross Defendant and moving party; Shannon M. Stone, of Millstein & Associates, (415) 348-0348, appearing for Defendant the Shemano Group, Inc. and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained without leave to amend. Cross-complainant fails to plead facts sufficient to establish that the relationships between the parties gives raise to a duty from cross-defendant to cross-complainant. No authority supports plaintiff's contention that the bank owed cross-complainant, who was not a recipient of any loan proceeds, a duty to exercise reasonable care in the underwriting of the loan to Del Biaggio. No facts are plead to suggest it would have been foreseeable to the bank that cross-complainant would be defrauded by its own employee (Cacchione) thereby exposing cross-complainant to liability to third parties. No reasonable probability that defect can be cured by amendment. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

AMENDED DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-14-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF/CROSS DEFENDANT PACIFIC CAPITAL BANK N.A., AMENDED DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Appearances: Shannon M. Stone, Millstein & Associates, (415) 348-0348, appearing for Defendant and cross complainant The Shemano Group; Shari D. Marks, of Frandzel Robins Bloom & Csato, L.D. (415) 788-7400 appearing for Cross Defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to the first cause of action for declaratory relief. The issues of the parties' liabilities under the control agreement are directly addressed in the complaint, thus the declaratory relief action is duplicative. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the second cause of action for negligence. Cross-complainant to plead the relationship between the parties that gives rise to a duty from cross-defendant to cross-complainant. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Court Reporter: Mary Ellen Scherer, CSR #5208
